methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Persona AI is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Nic Radford and Jerry Pratt, having raised $25M to date. The broader Robotics & Manufacturing sector includes one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private peers including 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics, though none provide sufficiently tight comparability at the same stage and valuation tier for reliable peer-median analysis.

The Stage-Progression method is the applicable triangulation approach here. Using the $25M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for Seed-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $62M. This method reflects the expectation that follow-on funding will require a material step-up from seed entry, though it does not incorporate company-specific traction or margin assumptions given limited data visibility.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $53M (low) to $72M (median $62M), centered on the Stage-Progression output. Key uncertainties include the absence of a robust same-stage peer cohort in this subsector, limiting external benchmarking; the degree of product validation and customer traction relative to peer companies at similar stages; and capital efficiency relative to sector norms. Given the single-method basis, the range should be treated as indicative rather than definitive. Subsequent rounds will benefit from revenue, unit economics, or strategic milestone data to narrow this band.
